title: The AI Apprentice Test
subtitle: Stage Checkpoint · AI Foundations
description: You've learned what AI can and cannot do. Now prove it. The Apprentice Test challenges you to apply your knowledge of AI limitations, safety, and real-world behavior. Three questions stand between you and the final stage.
objective: Answer at least 2 of 3 questions correctly to pass the checkpoint.

challenges.question: Which statement about AI hallucinations is correct?
options: Hallucinations only occur when the model is asked about the future
The model is aware when it hallucinates and marks those answers
The model presents false information with the same confidence as true information
Hallucinations are a sign of a broken model and should be reported

challenges.question: An AI system can currently access the internet and fetch real-time data without any special tools or integrations.
What is AI alignment?
options: Ensuring the AI's text is properly formatted
Making AI systems behave in accordance with human intentions and values
Aligning multiple AI models to work together
A technique for making AI faster

title: The Prompt Critic
subtitle: Stage Checkpoint · Prompt Engineering
description: Four stages of prompting knowledge tested and proven. Now face the Prompt Critic — a harsh judge who accepts only precise, well-structured thinking. Three questions remain. Show that you understand not just what to do, but why.
objective: Answer at least 2 of 3 questions correctly to pass the checkpoint.

challenges.question: A developer asks: 'Fix my code'. What critical element is missing from this prompt?
options: A polite tone
A goal, context, and the actual code
The developer's name
The programming language only

challenges.question: Providing examples of the desired input-output format in a prompt (few-shot prompting) reliably improves consistency of structured output.
You need an LLM to always return the same JSON structure across hundreds of API calls. What is the most important thing to include?
options: A request to 'be consistent'
The exact JSON schema with field names and types in the system prompt
A higher temperature setting
A larger context window

title: The Syntax Sentinel
subtitle: Stage Checkpoint · JavaScript Essentials
description: The Sentinel guards the path to Arrays and Objects, testing whether you can read code the way a computer does — tracking variables, following logic, and predicting what loops will produce. Answer carefully; the Sentinel doesn't accept guesses.
objective: Answer at least 2 of 3 questions correctly to pass the checkpoint.

challenges.question: What does `typeof "7"` return in JavaScript?
options: "number"
"string"
"boolean"

challenges.question: In JavaScript, `const` variables can be reassigned a new value after they're created.
What is the final value of `count` after `let count = 0; for (let i = 0; i < 3; i++) { count = count + 2; }`?

title: The Parallel Phantom
subtitle: Stage Checkpoint · Advanced Playwright
description: The Phantom splits into copies of itself, daring you to keep your tests straight when everything runs at once. Prove you understand fixtures, page objects, and what it really takes for tests to survive running side by side.
objective: Answer at least 2 of 3 questions correctly to pass the checkpoint.

challenges.question: What does the `page` fixture in a Playwright test represent?
options: The entire browser application
A single browser tab, isolated from other tests
A test data file
A CI/CD pipeline step

challenges.question: The Page Object Model means writing raw CSS selectors directly inside every test.
Why is generating unique test data (like appending a timestamp) important when running tests in parallel?
options: It makes the test file shorter
It prevents multiple parallel runs from colliding over the same shared data
It is required by Playwright's syntax
It disables retries

title: The Endpoint Enforcer
subtitle: Stage Checkpoint · APIs
description: The Enforcer guards every endpoint along the path to Authentication, checking whether you truly understand methods, status codes, and how data travels in a request. Slip up here, and it sends your request right back.
objective: Answer at least 2 of 3 questions correctly to pass the checkpoint.

challenges.question: Which HTTP method should you use to permanently remove a resource from a server?
options: GET
POST
DELETE
PATCH

challenges.question: A status code in the 5xx range means the problem is with the request you sent, not the server.
What does `fetch()` return when you call it?
options: The parsed JSON immediately
A promise that resolves to a response object
A string of HTML
An error

title: The Loop Guardian
subtitle: Stage Checkpoint · AI Agents
description: The Guardian cycles endlessly through think, act, and observe, and it won't step aside until you prove you understand that same loop — along with the tools, memory, and planning that keep an agent on track.
objective: Answer at least 2 of 3 questions correctly to pass the checkpoint.

challenges.question: What does the 'think, act, observe' loop let an agent do?
options: Generate longer responses
Repeatedly decide on, take, and learn from actions until a goal is reached
Avoid using any tools
Run without a language model

challenges.question: An agent's context window can hold an unlimited amount of information.
What is task decomposition?
options: Deleting parts of a goal that seem too hard
Breaking a large goal into smaller, manageable steps
Running a tool multiple times
Summarizing a conversation

title: The Protocol Phantom
subtitle: Stage Checkpoint · MCP Mastery
description: The Phantom slips between servers and clients, testing whether you really understand how MCP's pieces fit together — roles, structure, and the discipline a good server needs before it acts.
objective: Answer at least 2 of 3 questions correctly to pass the checkpoint.

challenges.question: In MCP, which role manages a connection to a single server from inside the host?
options: The resource
The client
The prompt
The model

challenges.question: MCP lets any compliant client communicate with any compliant server because they share a common protocol structure.
What should an MCP server do before carrying out a request with the arguments it received?
options: Run it immediately without checks
Validate that the arguments are present, well-formed, and within expected bounds
Forward it to a different server
Ignore it

title: The Architecture Sentinel
subtitle: Stage Checkpoint · Software Architecture
description: The Sentinel has watched countless systems grow, fracture, and scale — and it tests whether you can reason clearly about monoliths, layers, and the forces that push a system to grow beyond its original shape.
objective: Answer at least 2 of 3 questions correctly to pass the checkpoint.

challenges.question: What is a key characteristic of a monolithic application?
options: It splits every feature into its own deployable service
All of its functionality lives in a single deployable codebase and process
It cannot have a user interface
It must run across many servers

challenges.question: In a layered architecture, the data layer should typically depend on and know about the presentation layer.
What does 'horizontal scaling' mean?
options: Making a single machine more powerful
Adding more machines that share the load
Reducing the number of features in a system
Rewriting the system in a different language

title: The Workflow Wraith
subtitle: Stage Checkpoint · AI Productivity
description: The Wraith drifts between drafts, research threads, and half-finished workflows, testing whether you can tell a well-framed request from a vague one — and a good fit for AI from a task that calls for human judgment.
objective: Answer at least 2 of 3 questions correctly to pass the checkpoint.

challenges.question: What makes a request to an AI tool more likely to produce a useful result?
options: Keeping it as vague as possible
Including a clear goal, audience, tone, and any relevant constraints
Never specifying a format
Avoiding any mention of the topic

challenges.question: It's a good idea to verify important factual claims an AI makes before relying on them.
What kind of work task is generally a good fit for AI assistance?
options: A high-stakes decision with major consequences for people
A routine, low-stakes task that's easy to review afterward
A task involving confidential data with no review process
Nothing — AI should never assist with work

title: The Echo of Lessons Past
subtitle: Stage Checkpoint · AI Adventure Master Path
description: This checkpoint reflects fragments of everything you've learned so far back at you — prompting, automation, and the rigor of a trustworthy Playwright suite — to see whether the lessons have truly stuck.
objective: Answer at least 2 of 3 questions correctly to pass the checkpoint.

challenges.question: What three ingredients tend to make up a strong prompt?
options: A title, a signature, and a date
A clear goal, relevant context, and constraints like format or tone
Random keywords with no clear structure
Only the topic, with nothing else

challenges.question: A task that is frequent, predictable, and has a clear definition of 'done' tends to be a good candidate for automation.
What is the biggest risk when a test suite becomes unreliable?
options: It runs a few seconds slower
People stop trusting it, so it loses its value as a safety net
It automatically repairs itself over time
Nothing changes in practice
